title: Rootless bind mounts with SELinux are unchecked for privilege
body:
### Issue Description

If you create a bind mount with the `z` SELinux flag, Podman will naively attempt to run `lsetxattr` on the mount, even if it doesn't have permission to do so. 

That will cause the container to fail to start. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Add a service with a bind mount:

```yaml
services:
  some_service:
    image: some-image
    container_name: fooname
    environment:
      - SOME_PLACE
    volumes:
      - type: bind
        source: ${SOME_PLACE}
        target: /home/user/some_place
        read_only: false
        bind:
          propagation: rprivate
          selinux: z
```

2. Run `podman-compose up -d` (or some equal `podman run ...` command)

Please note that the problem also occurs with `podman unshare podman-compose up -d`.

### Describe the results you received

You'll get the following error:

```
Error: lsetxattr(label=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c27,c424) /home/barrett/Documents/some_place/some_place: operation not permitted
Error: unable to start container "04960032e3428a32ab30481438f5997d089dbf0008668d4bae17c2ac07c16d59": lsetxattr(label=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c27,c424) /home/barrett/Documents/some_place/some_place: operation not permitted
```

(yes, it says the folder name twice)

### Describe the results you expected

In an interactive shell, Podman could ask prompt for elevated permissions if it needs to make privileged changes. Something like `pkexec` or another elevation method would likely do the trick.

For non-interactive sessions, a couple informative diagnostics explaining the situation would be preferable.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

comments.body: How are we expected to change SELinux labels without permission to set xattrs? I think errors from requesting a relabel here seem expected?
If you tell podman to set SELinux labels on something and it gets ENUSUP or EPERM, it needs to fail, I don't see how this would work otherwise.  Similar if you told it to change UIDs.
I'm going to close this as expected behavior; if a relabel is requested without permission to do so on the directory in question, Podman must error.
The diagnostics here are somewhat poor. Would you be interested in a PR adding user-friendly diagnostics?
Improved error messages are always welcome. Were you thinking of something in addition to that?
